text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little village nestled between golden fields,

There lived an old lady named Matilda. She was known far and wide for her garden parties. Every evening, the village would gather around her large oak tree, where Matilda would regale them with mysterious tales of old.

One evening, she said, "Last week, a squirrel vanished."

"Perhaps it went on an extraordinary vacation?" one guessed.

"No, no," Matilda chuckled. "It got lost in the enchanted forest."

"What did it see?" someone inquired.

Matilda pondered. "A moonlit parade, with flowers singing."

"Sounds like a dream."

"No, they had lanterns. Old owl friends. It returned every full moon."

The villagers roared with laughter. Matilda nodded. "They have a kind of harmony I can't replicate."

"Perhaps," another chimed in, "We ought to have an owl concert."

"That would be musical."

"But never owl-themed
